## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

```

    }
```
```

```

content.thinking:
Let me understand the issue:

1. There's a bug in the `experimental:function-return-type-spacing` rule
2. When a function has a very long name and a long return type, the rule reformats it to put the return type on the same line as the function signature
3. This can cause the line to exceed the `max_line_length` limit

The example:
```kotlin
fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
    AndAlsoALongReturnType {

}
```

Gets reformatted to:
```kotlin
fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

}
```

And this violates the `max_line_length = 120` rule.

The issue mentions that the fix might need to:
- Make an exception for parameterless functions, OR
- Honor the max line length

Let me start by examining the relevant files.

107â†’        var lengthOfReturnType = 0

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8â†’
  9â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10â†’- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11â†’- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12â†’- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13â†’
 14â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15â†’
 16â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17â†’
 18â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19â†’* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20â†’* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22â†’
 23â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24â†’
 25â†’#### Experimental rules
 26â†’
 27â†’Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28â†’
 29â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30â†’
 31â†’#### EditorConfig
 32â†’
 33â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34â†’
 35â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36â†’
 37â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38â†’
 39â†’A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40â†’
 41â†’The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42â†’
 43â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46â†’
 47â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48â†’
 49â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50â†’
 51â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. ## Summary

I've successfully fixed the issue where the `experimental:function-return-type-spacing` rule could violate the `max-line-length` rule.

### Changes Made:

1. **Modified FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt**:
   - Added support for reading the `max_line_length` configuration from `.editorconfig`
   - Implemented a `wouldExceedMaxLineLength()` function that calculates whether reformatting a function signature would exceed the maximum line length
   - Updated `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType()` to skip reformatting when it would violate the max line length

2. **Created comprehensive tests**:
   - FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest.kt - Tests for max line length interaction
   - FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleEdgeCasesTest.kt - Additional edge case tests
   - All tests pass successfully

3. **Updated CHANGELOG.md** to document the fix

### How the Fix Works:

The rule now:
1. Checks if `max_line_length` is configured and enabled
2. When it encounters a function with a newline between the colon and return type, it calculates what the line length would be if reformatted
3. If the projected line length would exceed the configured maximum, it skips the reformatting and doesn't report a violation
4. If the line would fit within the limit, it proceeds with the normal reformatting

This ensures that the rule never produces output that would violate the max-line-length rule, while still enforcing proper spacing for functions that fit within the limit.

content.text:
Perfect! The fix is working correctly. Let me now write a summary of all the changes I've made:

## Summary

I've successfully fixed the issue where the `experimental:function-return-type-spacing` rule could violate the `max-line-length` rule.

### Changes Made:

#### 1. **Modified `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`**
   - Added support for reading the `max_line_length` configuration from `.editorconfig`
   - Added `usesEditorConfigProperties` to declare usage of `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY`
   - Added `beforeFirstNode()` to read the max line length configuration
   - Implemented `wouldExceedMaxLineLength()` function that:
     - Checks if max line length is enabled (not -1 or Int.MAX_VALUE)
     - Only applies when there's a newline after the colon
     - Calculates the projected line length if the return type were moved to the same line
     - Returns true if this would exceed the configured max line length
   - Updated `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType()` to check if reformatting would exceed max line length before reporting violations

#### 2. **Created Comprehensive Tests**
   - **FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest.kt**: Tests for max line length interaction
     - Test for long functions that would exceed max line length (no violation expected)
     - Test for short functions that wouldn't exceed max line length (violation expected)
     - Test for when max line length is disabled (always reformat)
   - **FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleEdgeCasesTest.kt**: Additional edge case tests
     - Functions already on one line
     - Multiple spaces or no space between colon and return type
     - Functions with parameters

#### 3. **Updated CHANGELOG.md**
   - Added entry in the "Fixed" section documenting the bug fix

### How the Fix Works:

The rule now intelligently handles max line length constraints:

1. **Before the fix**: The rule would blindly reformat all function signatures to have a single space between the colon and return type, potentially creating lines that exceeded `max_line_length`.

2. **After the fix**: The rule:
   - Checks if `max_line_length` is configured and enabled
   - When encountering a function with a newline between colon and return type, calculates the projected line length if reformatted
   - If the projected length would exceed the configured maximum, skips reformatting and reports no violation
   - If the line would fit within the limit, proceeds with normal reformatting

### Test Results:

âś… All existing tests pass  
âś… All new tests pass  
âś… Manual testing with the exact example from the issue confirms the fix works correctly

The fix ensures that the `function-return-type-spacing` rule never produces output that would violate the `max-line-length` rule, while still enforcing proper spacing for functions that fit within the configured limit.

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8â†’
  9â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10â†’- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11â†’- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12â†’- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13â†’
 14â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15â†’
 16â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17â†’
 18â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19â†’* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20â†’* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22â†’
 23â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24â†’
 25â†’#### Experimental rules
 26â†’
 27â†’Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28â†’
 29â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30â†’
 31â†’#### EditorConfig
 32â†’
 33â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34â†’
 35â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36â†’
 37â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38â†’
 39â†’A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40â†’
 41â†’The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42â†’
 43â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46â†’
 47â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48â†’
 49â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50â†’
 51â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52â†’

235â†’### Fixed
236â†’
237â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
238â†’* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
239â†’* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
240â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
241â†’* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756)).
